full_text: <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> There are stories from our lives that tell others a lot about us.<|7.00|> <|7.00|> How we move the world, how we respond to situations,<|12.00|> <|12.00|> stories that tell others about our priorities and our motivations.<|18.00|> <|18.00|> Our actions and reactions reveal so much about us.<|24.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> When our youngest child was about seven,<|4.00|> <|4.00|> I was frantically trying to place my order for our Christmas cards<|9.00|> <|9.00|> before a really good coupon expired.<|14.00|> <|14.00|> And I did it, and I was so pleased with myself.<|18.00|> <|18.00|> Three young children, so many things to juggle,<|21.00|> <|21.00|> and I was ahead of the holiday rush.<|24.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I was feeling rather smug and accomplished.<|4.44|> <|4.44|> The cards arrived about two weeks later, and they looked great.<|9.02|> <|9.02|> A sweet picture of our family on the front and a cheery holiday message with our names<|15.12|> <|15.12|> printed inside.<|18.00|> <|18.00|> Merry Christmas from the Pearsons.<|20.46|> <|20.46|> Don, Katie, William, Bess, and Dixie.<|25.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Well, for those of you who don't know everyone in my family, that list includes the names<|7.30|> <|7.30|> of only two of my children, not three, and the dog.<|15.34|> <|15.34|> I forgot Owen.<|19.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And in my haste to make the coupon deadline, I left Owen's name off of our card.<|7.76|> <|7.76|> I remember to include the dog's name, but not our son's.<|14.76|> <|14.76|> To be clear, Owen was in the picture on the front of the card.<|20.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And this may surprise some of you, but it doesn't surprise anyone in my family, I still used them.<|8.00|> <|8.00|> I mean, friends, I had gotten a great deal, and I was not going to waste them.<|17.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> So I told Owen that he could add a note to each card. He could dictate it. I would type it up. We'd put a little slip of paper in every card. He could say whatever he wanted as compensation for my mistake.<|15.88|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And that slip of paper read, "This is Owen. I am not dead. I am still a part of this family.<|13.90|> <|13.90|> My mom had a coupon and would not reprint the cards.<|18.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I got 30 extra minutes of computer time because of this.<|7.40|> <|7.40|> Merry Christmas.<|9.94|> <|9.94|> There's a lot that you can learn about both of us from this story.<|20.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I am prone to make mistakes when I am rushing up against a deadline.<|6.00|> <|6.00|> I am frugal and I love a deal.<|10.00|> <|10.00|> I also hate waste.<|12.00|> <|12.00|> And I am not above bribing my children.<|15.00|> <|15.00|> And you also learn that<|19.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Owen was, and I will say continues to be, gracious and forgiving beyond my deserving.<|10.24|> <|10.24|> Owen, a classic third child, rolls with the punches.<|15.60|> <|15.60|> And Owen is easily bribed.<|18.88|> <|18.88|> And he needed to up his game on his negotiating skills because I would have given so much<|24.48|> <|24.48|> more than 30 minutes.<|27.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|3.98|> We collect and cherish these memories<|8.04|> <|8.04|> and stories and families, don't we?<|10.76|> <|11.84|> We only have to say a key word, Owen and the Christmas card,<|15.46|> <|15.46|> and the whole family laughs and chimes in<|18.40|> <|18.40|> with bits of the event from their perspective,<|21.76|> <|21.76|> all talking over each other<|23.88|> <|23.88|> and adding their own unique bits to the narrative.<|27.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> These are our touchstones and how we know and love each other as families and communities.<|10.18|> <|10.18|> Through our shared memories and our are presented with stories of Jesus that give us insight into who he is.<|14.92|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It's a season of wonder and discovery, and we are presented with identity through stories of miracles,<|22.88|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> else.<|1.02|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> and teachings throughout the entire Epiphany season.<|3.58|> <|3.58|> And friends, Epiphany is my favorite season.<|6.94|> <|6.94|> Maybe it's that Nancy Drew, I love a mystery thing,<|10.18|> <|10.18|> I don't know.<|11.62|> <|11.62|> But this year, we get a really long and spacious Epiphany<|16.62|> <|17.82|> because Easter's so good.<|18.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> late. Last week we learned about a mother and son relationship through the story of the wedding at<|10.00|> <|10.00|> Cana. And this week in Luke's gospel, we encounter Jesus giving what some have called his first sermon<|19.76|> <|19.76|> or his inaugural address of his ministry in a synagogue in his hometown of Nazareth.<|27.56|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The writer of Luke seems to have adopted the Aristotelian triptych, I don't know if you<|7.00|> <|7.00|> all know that term, but I learned it in speech class, for this gospel.<|13.64|> <|13.64|> His methodology for unveiling Jesus to us is, "Tell them what you're going to tell them.<|20.24|> <|20.24|> Tell them.<|21.24|> <|21.24|> Tell them what you told them."<|24.04|> <|24.04|> That is Luke's formula.<|27.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.88|> We hear the echoes of Mary's song of praise from Luke 1 in today's text.<|9.52|> <|10.42|> The Magnificat told us he has shown strength with his arm.<|14.88|> <|15.00|> He's scattered the proud in the thoughts of their hearts.<|17.78|> <|17.78|> He has empty.<|7.22|> <|9.18|> Fast forward, and as Jesus reads from Isaiah in the synagogue in our text today,<|15.06|> <|15.54|> the words tell us what Mary already foretold about who Jesus is.<|24.28|> the captives and recovery of sight to the blind,<|14.76|> <|15.64|> to let the oppressed go free, to proclaim the year of the Lord's favor.<|21.78|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.20|> And this is Luke's version of John's many more direct I am statements, because Jesus concludes,<|11.44|> <|12.24|> today this scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing.<|17.82|> <|18.70|> I am the fulfillment.<|21.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.74|> Jesus has just announced plainly who he is and confirmed what Mary said before he was even born,<|11.78|> <|13.80|> telling us see Jesus fully embody his ministry of healing, teaching, releasing,<|17.62|> <|18.46|> and bringing about the kingdom of God.<|20.92|> <|21.92|> Not at some point in the future, but here and creating a fuller picture of Jesus' identity.<|14.12|> <|15.62|> I know I've shared this quotation or this idea with you before because I love it so much.<|22.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> In her book, An Altar in the World, Barbara Brown Taylor encourages us to become detectives of the divine.<|11.60|> <|13.00|> And that is the
